zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Parsons於1587年9月10日出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可易普斯維其時，她的父親John Parsons20歲，母親Mary Coman13歲。她於1616年5月5日與Thomas Powell I在英國英格蘭素福爾可科華士 希斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1621年9月10日在英國英格蘭素福爾可科華士 希斯享年34歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Parsons于1587年9月10日出生在英国英格兰素福爾可易普斯維其时，她的父亲John Parsons20岁，母亲Mary Coman13岁。她于1616年5月5日与Thomas Powell I在英国英格兰素福爾可科華士 希斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1621年9月10日在英国英格兰素福爾可科華士 希斯享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: Parsons
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for the servant of a parish priest or parson, from the possessive case of Middle English persone, parsoun (see Parson ).

English: many early examples are found with the prepositions (e.g. Ralph del Persones 1323); these are habitational names, with the omission of house, hence in effect occupational names for servants employed at the parson's house.
English: post-medieval variant of Parson , with excrescent -s. Alternatively, Parson may be a shortened form of Parsons.

variants.name: Passon
meanings: Germanized form of Polish Pasoń or Pason, a derivative of pas ‘belt’. The Germanized form is found in both Poland and Germany.
English: perhaps an altered form of Parson; see Parsons . This surname is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Passons
meanings: English: probably an altered form of   Parsons  .

variants.name: Parson
meanings: English: status name or nickname from Middle English persone, persoun ‘priest, parson’ (Old French persone). The status of medieval bearers of the surname is uncertain. Priests were generally known by a habitational name rather than by a surname denoting their role in the church. They were not allowed to marry, but doubtless many had illegitimate children, some of whom may have been given Parson as a surname. Some of the original bearers may have been given it as a nickname (compare Priest , Bishop , Monk ) or it may have been an occupational name for someone who worked for the parson (a shortened form of Personesservaunt perhaps).
English: in northern England and the North Midlands especially, it is often a variant of   Pearson  . For the change in vowel compare   Parr  .
Americanized form of Swedish Pärsson, Pehrsson (see Pehrson ) or Persson , patronymics from vernacular shortened forms of the personal name Peter .

variants.name: Persons
meanings: Perhaps an Americanized form of Dutch Persoons, Persoens, a patronymic from Middle Dutch persoon ‘parson’, probably denoting the servant of a parish priest, or possibly an altered form of English Parsons .
English (Wiltshire and Yorkshire): variant of   Parsons  . This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: McPherson
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac a' Phearsain ‘son of the parson or priest’ (see Parsons ).

History: This is the name of a Highland Scottish clan, part of the Chattan confederation, the founder of which is believed to have been a man named Muireach or Murdo Cattenach, priest of Kingussie in Badenoch in the 9th century.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Parsons was born on 10 September 1587, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Parsons, was 20 and her mother, Mary Coman, was 13. She married Thomas Powell I on 5 May 1616, in Forest Heath, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 10 September 1621, in Forest Heath, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 34.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Parsons(이)가 1587년 9월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 이프스위치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Parsons(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Coman(은)는 13세였다. 그녀는 1616년 5월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 포레스트 히스에서 Thomas Powell I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1621년 9월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 포레스트 히스에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Parsons nasceu em 10 de setembro de 1587, em Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Parsons, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Mary Coman, tinha 13 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Powell I em 5 de maio de 1616, em Forest Heath, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 10 de setembro de 1621, em Forest Heath, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 34 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Parsons родилась 10 сентября 1587 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Parsons было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Coman, 13. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Powell I 5 мая 1616 г. в местности форест хит, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 10 сентября 1621 г. в местности форест хит, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 34 лет.
